text: Last summer, I finally took a trip I had been planning for months to Japan. Before leaving, I researched popular spots and learned a few basic phrases, which really helped. Experiencing a new culture was incredible—from visiting ancient temples to trying street food like takoyaki and ramen. The food was a highlight; each meal felt like an adventure. However, there were some language barriers, especially when ordering in small local restaurants. A smile and a few words of Japanese usually solved things. Despite the challenges, the memories I made—like watching a sunset over Kyoto—are unforgettable. This trip taught me that even simple planning can open doors to wonderful experiences.
